title: Creative block after career change
story: I left my stable corporate job six months ago to become a freelance graphic designer. I had been building a portfolio for two years and felt ready, but now that I'm actually doing it full-time, I can't seem to produce anything I'm proud of. Every project feels derivative, and I keep comparing my early drafts to the established designers I admire. I've turned down paid work because I didn't think I could deliver, and I'm burning through my savings. My former colleagues tell me I'm talented and just need time, but I worry I made a huge mistake and that I'm not actually good enough to make this work.
